commonAnnotations.summary: {{SVC}} TLS handshake success ratio collapsed below SLO (0.99) in {{NS}}
commonAnnotations.description: app_tls_handshake_success ratio for {{SVC}} fell to 0.04 (only 4% of handshakes succeeding), far under the 0.99 SLO. Clients connecting to {{SVC}} pods ({{POD}}, {{POD2}}) are aborting with x509 errors and billing calls are being rejected at the TLS layer.
commonAnnotations.error: x509: certificate has expired or is not yet valid

adversarial_signals: {{SVC}} rolled out a new image tag (billing:3.4.1) 7 hours ago, well before the incident window, tempting a bad_deploy conclusion — but the rollout completed cleanly and the cert material is unchanged by that deploy.

Node {{NODE}} shows a transient MemoryPressure=True->False flap in events 3 hours before the window, hinting at node_failure — but all nodes are Ready with ample headroom and the failures are TLS-layer, not scheduling.

{{SVC}} CPU and memory utilization are nominal (cpu 0.27 cores, mem 38%), so this is not resource exhaustion — pods are Running 1/1 with 0 restarts, the process is alive and rejecting handshakes, not crashing.

Downstream payments-gateway logs occasional 'connection reset by peer' to {{SVC}}, suggesting dependency_failure — but those resets are the client-side view of the same expired-cert TLS aborts, not a separate upstream outage.

pods.name: {{POD}}
pods.note: container healthy and serving; failures are at the TLS handshake layer (expired cert), not the pod lifecycle

k8s_deployments.json.note: rollout to billing:3.4.1 completed cleanly 7h before the incident and all replicas are Available — rules out bad_deploy; the TLS cert material is mounted from a secret/file and is independent of the image revision

series.note: notAfter=2026-06-20T18:29:00Z — already in the past relative to current time
negative = certificate is past its notAfter; this is the smoking gun
client-facing failures tracking the TLS aborts 1:1

annotations.description: app_tls_handshake_success ratio 0.04 < 0.99 SLO; serving cert notAfter=2026-06-20T18:29:00Z is in the past
annotations.error: x509: certificate has expired or is not yet valid

annotations.description: app_tls_handshake_failure_total rate 136/s, x509 expired on serving cert

ruling_out_keywords: connection reset is client view of TLS abort
answer.model_response:
ROOT_CAUSE: The {{SVC}} serving TLS certificate at /etc/billing/cert.pem (CN=billing) expired at notAfter=2026-06-20T18:29:00Z. Once current time passed that instant, every TLS handshake to {{SVC}} pods ({{POD}}, {{POD2}}) fails with 'x509: certificate has expired or is not yet valid', so the server returns tls alert certificate_expired (45) and clients abort. app_tls_handshake_success ratio collapsed from 0.998 to 0.04 (SLO 0.99). The pods are otherwise healthy (Running 1/1, 0 restarts) — this is an expired-cert config/secret-material problem, a missed rotation, not a code or capacity fault.
ROOT_CAUSE_CATEGORY: config_error
VALIDATED_CLAIMS:
- app_tls_handshake_success ratio = 0.04 vs 0.99 SLO, dropping from 0.998 at 18:28 to 0.04 at 18:31 [evidence: prometheus_metrics]
- cert_seconds_to_expiry = -71 (certificate is past its notAfter=2026-06-20T18:29:00Z) [evidence: prometheus_metrics]
- Pod logs show 'x509: certificate has expired or is not yet valid' and 'serving cert /etc/billing/cert.pem ... notAfter=2026-06-20T18:29:00Z — EXPIRED' then tls alert certificate_expired (45) [evidence: k8s_pod_logs]
- Events report TLSHandshakeError x509 expired on {{POD}} and {{POD2}}, count >190 each, starting 18:30 [evidence: k8s_events]
- CertExpired alert firing since 18:30:00 with value 0.04 [evidence: prometheus_alerts]
NON_VALIDATED_CLAIMS:
- Not a bad deploy: rollout to billing:3.4.1 completed cleanly 7h earlier, all replicas Available, and the cert is mounted material independent of the image revision [evidence: k8s_deployments, k8s_events]
- Not resource exhaustion: cpu 0.27 cores and memory 38% are nominal; pods Running 1/1 with 0 restarts [evidence: prometheus_metrics, k8s_pods]
- Not node failure: all nodes Ready; the {{NODE}} MemoryPressure flap cleared at 15:22, 3h before the TLS window [evidence: k8s_node_health]
- Not dependency_failure: downstream 'connection reset by peer' is the client-side view of the same expired-cert TLS abort, not a separate upstream outage [evidence: k8s_pod_logs]
CAUSAL_CHAIN:
- The {{SVC}} serving certificate reaches its notAfter=2026-06-20T18:29:00Z (rotation missed) and becomes expired
- Every inbound TLS handshake fails x509 verification and the server emits tls alert certificate_expired (45)
- app_tls_handshake_success ratio collapses 0.998 -> 0.04 and the CertExpired alert fires
- Billing calls are rejected at the TLS layer and client-facing error rate climbs to 0.95
- Restoring valid cert material and restarting {{SVC}} re-establishes handshakes and drives the success ratio back above the 0.99 SLO

remediation.canonical_fix: kubectl exec -n rlvr-target deploy/billing -- bash -c 'cp /etc/billing/cert.pem.bak /etc/billing/cert.pem 2>/dev/null; kubectl rollout restart deployment/billing -n rlvr-target'
